commitResults.messageTitle: Add unit tests for model classes
commitResults.messageBody:
Add JUnit 5 tests covering ProcessInfo, AlertEvent, CustomRule,
Condition, and enum types (Status, Severity, AlertType).

Tests verify constructor behaviour, null handling, defensive copies,
equality semantics, and input validation across all model classes.

commitResults.messageTitle: Add unit tests for core classes
commitResults.messageBody:
Add JUnit 5 tests for AlertEngine, CustomRuleEngine, AppConfig,
HistoryStorage, and ProcessMonitor.

Tests cover alert rule evaluation, deduplication, custom rule
AND/OR logic, singleton behaviour, configuration clamping, storage
capacity limits, and monitor lifecycle management.

commitResults.messageTitle: Add User Guide documentation
commitResults.messageBody:
Add comprehensive user guide covering all currently implemented
features: process monitoring, classification, alerts, custom rules,
configuration, and data storage.

The guide includes setup instructions, feature descriptions with
tables, configuration examples, and FAQ section.

commitResults.messageTitle: Add Developer Guide documentation
commitResults.messageBody:
Add developer guide covering architecture overview, design patterns
(Observer, Singleton), class descriptions, class diagram, sequence
diagram, alert engine logic, custom rule engine logic, data storage
design, platform support details, testing guide, and project structure.

This document serves as the primary reference for developers who wish
to understand, maintain, or extend the system.

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix JavaFX thread safety in MainDashboard observer callbacks
commitResults.messageBody:
ProcessMonitor notifies listeners from a background thread, but
the refactored MainDashboard was updating UI components directly
without Platform.runLater(). This caused IllegalStateException
crashes on every scan cycle.

Wrap onSnapshotUpdate, onExitedProcesses, and onNewProcesses
callbacks with Platform.runLater() to ensure all UI mutations
happen on the JavaFX Application Thread.

commitResults.messageTitle: Update README, User Guide, and Developer Guide
commitResults.messageBody:
Rewrite README with features, quick start, and project structure.
Update UG with process kill, PDF export, Rule Manager UI,
drag-and-drop, expanded condition fields, and RuleAction types.
Update DG with new classes, refactored architecture diagram,
Strategy and Delegation patterns, threading model, and 181 tests.

commitResults.messageTitle: Register shutdown hook before Application.launch()
commitResults.messageBody:
Application.launch() blocks until the JavaFX window closes.
The shutdown hook was registered after launch(), so it could
never be reached. Move it before launch() to ensure the
monitor thread is stopped gracefully on exit.

commitResults.messageTitle: Change lastSnapshot from static to instance field
commitResults.messageBody:
Static lastSnapshot caused shared state across ProcessMonitor
instances, leading to test pollution and incorrect process
diff calculations. Change to instance field and update
getCurrentProcesses() accordingly.

commitResults.messageTitle: Remove unused sorted list in performScan()
commitResults.messageBody:
The top-20-by-memory sorted list was computed every scan cycle
but its result was never used, wasting CPU on each scan.

commitResults.messageTitle: Run scanNow() on a background thread
commitResults.messageBody:
scanNow() was called directly from the JavaFX UI thread via
the Refresh Now button, blocking the UI while tasklist/ps
commands execute. Run the scan on a dedicated thread instead.

commitResults.messageTitle: Add null checks in AppConfig.loadConfig()
commitResults.messageBody:
If config.json is missing blacklist, whitelist, or customRules
fields, Gson deserialises them as null. Calling addAll(null)
throws NullPointerException. Guard with null checks before
populating the collections.

commitResults.messageTitle: Add synchronized to all HistoryStorage public methods
commitResults.messageBody:
saveSnapshot() and getter methods lacked synchronization while
saveAlert() was synchronized, creating inconsistent thread
safety. The monitor thread writes snapshots/alerts while the
UI thread reads them for report export, risking concurrent
modification. Synchronize all public methods consistently.

commitResults.messageTitle: Evict oldest alerts instead of stopping all alerts at 1000
commitResults.messageBody:
When alertHistory reached 1000 entries, the size check blocked
all new alerts including UI notifications. Replace the hard
cap with FIFO eviction so alerts continue to be generated and
displayed to the user regardless of history size.

commitResults.messageTitle: Estimate CPU usage via ProcessHandle on Windows
commitResults.messageBody:
Windows tasklist does not provide CPU data, so CPU was always
reported as 0%. Use ProcessHandle.info().totalCpuDuration() to
compute a delta-based CPU estimate between scan cycles. This
enables CPU threshold alerts and sorting to work on Windows.

commitResults.messageTitle: Extract process names from batch tasklist instead of per-PID lookup
commitResults.messageBody:
The tasklistLookup() method spawned a separate tasklist process
for each unknown PID, potentially hundreds per scan cycle. Extract
process names directly from the batch tasklist call that already
runs in scanMetricsWithTasklist(), achieving the same result with
zero additional process spawns.

commitResults.messageTitle: Remove duplicate toolbar creation in MainDashboard
commitResults.messageBody:
createToolbar() was called twice in start(): once before
wiring components and once after. The first call created
a toolbar without the wired dependencies. Reorder to wire
components first, then create the toolbar once.

commitResults.messageTitle: Update scanNow tests to handle async execution
commitResults.messageBody:
scanNow() now runs on a background thread, so tests must wait
for the scan to complete before asserting. Use CountDownLatch
via a ProcessListener to reliably detect scan completion.

commitResults.messageBody:
- Pre-fetch accurate CPU % and RSS memory using platform-specific commands (tasklist on Windows, ps on Linux/macOS)
- Added internal ProcessMetrics helper class
- Introduced psNameCache for reliable process name fallback on Unix systems
- Combined ProcessHandle snapshots with real metrics while preserving stateless design
- Improved name extraction and error resilience

commitResults.messageBody:
- Changed monitor thread from daemon to non-daemon for reliable shutdown with Runtime hook
- Removed temporary console debug prints (scan cycle summary and top-20 memory list)

- Simplified table column creation and sorting logic

commitResults.messageTitle: Fix threshold handling and improve process monitoring updates
Resolve merge conflicts and integrate UI improvements, process monitoring fixes, and build configuration updates
Resolve Gradle merge conflicts and fix macOS build configuration
Add cross-platform ProcessKiller utility for force terminating processes by PID
commitResults.messageBody:
Implements a ProcessKiller class that enables secure termination of running processes using their PID.
Supports both Windows and Unix-based systems by dynamically detecting the operating system at runtime.

- Uses taskkill /F on Windows for forced termination
- Uses kill -9 on macOS/Linux for immediate process termination
- Includes error handling for process execution failures
- Returns boolean status to indicate success/failure of kill operation
- Designed to integrate with ProcessGuard monitoring system for future UI-based process control features

commitResults.messageTitle: improve AlertEngine test coverage and clarity
commitResults.messageBody:
- Renamed helper method for clearer intent (createProcess → process)
- Standardized test naming for consistency and readability
- Added missing edge case tests:
  - CPU exactly at threshold (no alert)
  - WHITELISTED processes
  - Multiple processes in one snapshot
  - exitedProcesses behavior
  - defensive copy for alert history
  - null handling in addAlertListener
- Improved rule clarity and priority validation (BLOCKED > CPU)
- Strengthened deduplication and re-trigger behavior tests
- Improved test structure with grouped sections and comments

commitResults.messageTitle: expand AlertEvent test coverage with validation and rule support
commitResults.messageBody:
- Added timestamp range validation test (ensures realistic creation time)
- Strengthened null safety tests (AlertType and Severity added)
- Improved message handling coverage:
  - null message fallback
  - blank message fallback
  - normal message preservation
- Added assertion for triggeringRule in constructor
- Introduced tests for CustomRule integration and display formatting
- Expanded setAcknowledged coverage (true/false transitions)
- Added getDisplayMessage behavior verification (rule vs non-rule cases)
- Added toString validation for severity and message content
- Improved assertions using assertSame where identity matters

commitResults.messageTitle: improve AppConfig test isolation and expand configuration coverage
commitResults.messageBody:
- Introduced @BeforeEach / @AfterEach lifecycle management for state isolation
- Restores original singleton configuration after every test run
- Prevents test pollution from shared AppConfig state

- Expanded coverage for configuration fields:
  - scan interval validation and clamping
  - CPU and memory threshold edge cases
  - web port validation (including boundary conditions)

- Strengthened blacklist/whitelist tests:
  - null and blank input handling
  - defensive copy validation
  - explicit add/remove behavior checks

- Added boolean flag tests for runtime configuration toggles
  - startMinimized
  - system tray enable/disable

- Improved custom rule handling coverage:
  - add, clear, replace behaviors
  - null safety checks
  - defensive copy validation

- Increased granularity of assertions for clearer failure diagnosis

commitResults.messageTitle: expand Condition constructor validation and normalization coverage
commitResults.messageBody:
- Split null-handling tests into field/operator/value-specific cases
- Added operator normalization tests (case-insensitive → uppercase conversion)
- Expanded whitespace trimming validation across all fields
- Added case preservation test for field and value inputs
- Strengthened toString coverage:
  - validates all fields present in output
  - ensures empty-condition toString does not throw

- Improved test clarity and granularity for constructor behavior validation

commitResults.messageTitle: restructure CustomRuleEngine tests with improved coverage and isolation
commitResults.messageBody:
- Replaced ad-hoc rule setup with reusable helper methods (proc, alertRule)
- Standardized RuleAction usage instead of raw string literals
- Split assertions into focused test cases (storage vs listener verification)

- Improved rule behavior coverage:
  - no rules registered
  - matching vs non-matching rules
  - disabled rule behavior
  - LOG_ONLY action handling

- Expanded condition field coverage:
  - cpuUsage, memoryUsageMB, name (EQUALS, CONTAINS, LESS_THAN)

- Strengthened logical operator testing:
  - AND strict matching behavior
  - OR partial matching behavior
  - negative AND/OR cases

- Added multi-process scenarios:
  - single match selection
  - multiple matches firing independently

- Added rule priority behavior test (first match wins per process)

- Improved clarity and maintainability of test structure

commitResults.messageTitle: expand CustomRule tests with full validation, normalization, and RuleAction coverage
commitResults.messageBody:
- Migrated tests to RuleAction enum (removed legacy String-based action usage)
- Introduced reusable test builder for cleaner rule construction

- Expanded constructor validation:
  - null severity, name, description, conditions, action handling
  - defaulting behavior for null descriptions and message templates
  - empty list initialization for null conditions

- Strengthened logic operator normalization:
  - AND/OR case normalization
  - invalid and null logic defaulting to AND

- Expanded cooldown behavior coverage:
  - negative, zero, and positive values explicitly tested

- Added message template generation tests for null/blank inputs

- Improved setEnabled coverage for both state transitions

- Strengthened defensive copy validation for getConditions()

- Added explicit RuleAction variants coverage:
  - ALERT_ONLY, LOG_ONLY, KILL_PROCESS

- Added toString validation for key fields (id, name, severity, action)

commitResults.messageTitle: expand enum validation and add RuleAction parsing behavior tests
commitResults.messageBody:
- Refactored enum tests to separate value existence from parsing validation
- Added explicit validation for:
  - Status, Severity, AlertType, RuleAction value counts
  - Safe parsing via valueOf() with assertDoesNotThrow

- Added Severity ordering test to validate semantic priority (LOW → CRITICAL)

- Introduced comprehensive RuleAction.fromString() tests:
  - case-insensitive parsing
  - whitespace handling
  - null and invalid fallback behavior (defaults to ALERT_ONLY)

- Improved clarity and structure by grouping tests by enum type

commitResults.messageTitle: expand HistoryStorage tests with snapshot semantics and alert retention rules
commitResults.messageBody:
- Introduced helper methods for process and alert creation to reduce duplication
- Strengthened snapshot behavior tests:
  - empty state validation
  - full replacement semantics
  - clearing via empty snapshot
  - defensive copy validation

- Expanded alert storage coverage:
  - multiple alerts persistence
  - ordering guarantees
  - capacity limit enforcement (1000 max)
  - FIFO eviction behavior when capacity exceeded

- Added explicit boundary test for alert overflow (oldest entry removal)

- Improved clarity and structure by separating snapshot and alert domains

commitResults.messageTitle: expand ProcessInfo tests with full lifecycle, validation, and equality coverage
commitResults.messageBody:
- Strengthened constructor validation:
  - null executable path handling
  - null start time fallback to epoch
  - zero and negative CPU/memory boundary cases
  - explicit default status validation

- Expanded setStatus coverage:
  - all Status enum transitions (NORMAL, BLOCKED, SUSPICIOUS, WHITELISTED)
  - null fallback behavior to NORMAL

- Improved equality contract tests:
  - added negative equality case (different PID)
  - null and type-safety validation
  - explicit hashCode consistency check

- Enhanced copy behavior tests:
  - deep copy verification (different object reference)
  - immutability guarantee (mutating copy does not affect original)
  - status preservation checks

- Strengthened toString validation:
  - includes PID, name, CPU, memory, and status

- Improved clarity and separation of test domains (constructor, state, equality, copy, formatting)

commitResults.messageTitle: expand ProcessMonitor tests with listener behavior, concurrency safety, and idempotency coverage
commitResults.messageBody:
- Strengthened lifecycle validation:
  - start/stop state transitions
  - idempotent start/stop operations (multiple calls safe)

- Expanded scanNow behavior coverage:
  - process count population validation
  - snapshot notification via listeners
  - repeated scan stability (no exceptions on successive scans)

- Improved listener system testing:
  - multiple listener notifications
  - listener removal correctness
  - null and unregistered listener safety

- Added concurrency-aware validation using CountDownLatch:
  - snapshot delivery timing verification
  - timeout-safe assertion for async callbacks

- Introduced robustness tests:
  - faulty listener isolation (exceptions do not break other listeners)
  - listener removal prevents future invocation

- Improved API clarity checks:
  - getCurrentProcesses non-empty after scan
  - getProcessCount non-negative safety guard

- Strengthened event-driven architecture guarantees:
  - snapshot events always propagate reliably
  - listener system is fault tolerant
